def: adj. Living, alive.
v.n. To live ( = jīnā, q.v.).
s.f. A feast, entertainment, &c. = jeʼonār, q.v.
s.m. = jīvṛā, or jiyūṛā, q.v.
s.m. Life, soul; beloved object, sweetheart; will, pleasure, wish; — subsistence allowance, or annual payment (generally in grain) to village washermen and others.
s.f. = jītiyā, q.v.
s.m. Life, soul ( = jī); beloved, dear, darling.
s.m. pl. of next, q.v.
v.t. = jilānā, q.v.
v.t. (Braj) = jiyānā; jilānā; q.v.

ety: jiyā, q.v.+rā = Prk. डो = S. र+कः
def: s.m. (poet.) = jiyā or jī, and jiyarā, qq.v.

ety: S. जीव+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Life, soul; beloved, dear, darling ( = jīvṛā).
s.m. = jītab or jītav, q.v.
adj. Offended, displeased, put out
subs: v.n. To fume, fret, rave helplessly.

ety: for A. جزية jizyat, fem.; rt. جزي 'to give as a satisfaction,' &c.
def: s.m. A capitation-tax levied on the non-Muslim subjects of a Mohammadan government; (local) a house-tax on the inhabitants of towns not engaged in tillage.

s.m. Knowledge; understanding, intellect, intelligence; sacred or religious knowledge (such as is derived from meditation on the higher truths of religion and philosophy, and which teaches man his own nature, and how he may be reunited to the supreme spirit); cognizance; consciousness
subs: (jnāna+adh˚), s.m. Superiority or pre-eminence in intelligence
s.f. The pursuit or practice of wisdom
s.m. The eye of intelligence, mind's eye; intellectual vision
s.m. 'Mirror of true knowledge'; a man of Manjuśrī, one of the Jain saints
s.f. Clear vision or perception (untainted either by ignorance or by sin)
s.f. A state of intelligence or knowledge
s.m. That inner or esoteric portion of the Veda which relates to true spiritual knowledge or the knowledge of supreme spirit as distinguished from the knowledge of ceremonies
jnān-vān, adj. & s.m. Endowed with knowledge or science; knowing, familiar with, intelligent, wise, learned; having spiritual knowledge; — one who understands fully, &c. ( = jnānī, q.v.)
s.m. The thing called jnān, q.v.
s.m. Sacred and miscellaneous knowledge; the Vedas with the supplementary branches of knowledge, medicine, arms, &c.
(jnāna+ić˚), adj. Desirous of knowledge or wisdom
(jnāna+in˚), s.m. An organ of perception or sensation (of which there are five, viz., the skin, tongue, eye, ear, and nose); mental power, intellect.

def: adj. & s.m. Endowed with knowledge or intelligence, intelligent, wise, knowing, learned, sage, judicious; — one who understands fully; one endowed with religious knowledge; one who knows future events; a sage; a prognosticator, astrologer, fortune-teller (syn. jnān-wān).

adj. & s.m. Endowed with knowledge or intelligence, intelligent, wise, knowing, learned, sage, judicious; — one who understands fully; one endowed with religious knowledge; one who knows future events; a sage; a prognosticator, astrologer, fortune-teller (syn. jnān-wān).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Making or causing to know, teaching, informing, giving information, proclaiming; — a teacher, instructor; one who heralds or makes known.
s.m. Making known, announcing, notifying, apprizing, informing; teaching; announcement, notification, information
subs: adv. For the purpose of apprizing, &c.

def: part. Made known, informed, notified; revealed; discovered.
part. Made known, informed, taught, expounded.
s.f. Knowing; understanding, apprehension, comprehension; the exercise of the intellectual faculty.
adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent; acquainted (with); — one who knows, a knower, a wise or intelligent person; an acquaintance.
part. Known, ascertained, comprehended, understood
subs: (jnāta+an˚), s.m. 'Of known lineage'; a name of Vardhamān, the last Jin or Jain pontiff
s.m. A man completely versed in any science or śāstra.

def: adj. To be known or understood; to be investigated or inquired into; perceptible; conceivable; to be considered as.
s.m. A father; a paternal relation; a kinsman; a distant kinsman (one who does not participate in the oblations offered to deceased ancestors).
adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent; acquainted (with); — one who knows, a knower, a wise or intelligent person; an acquaintance.

ety: Prk. जेम्व or जेंव, fr. S. यावत्
def: adv. When ( = jab, q.v.)
subs: adv. (correl. wǒhīṅ), The very moment that, as soon as, no sooner.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

def: conj. If, if that, that; in that, inasmuch, since
subs: Though, although ( = agarći); inasmuch as, whereas ( = ćūṅki).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

ety: Ap. Prk. जेवडु (by elision of ड or change of ड to ण); Prk.जेद्दहो; S. याट्टश+कः
def: pron. Who, which, that, what
subs: The thing which; whatever thing, whatever
Whatever happens; at all events, at any rate
Whatever can be done, what is practicable or possible
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

def: s.m. Youth, youthfulness, the prime of life, bloom of youth; puberty, manhood, adolescence; vigour, bloom, flourishing state; the pleasures of youth; exquisite enjoyment; the pleasures of sense, taste, or culture; (met.) the breast, well-developed breasts; a fine figure; beauty
subs: joban umaknā, joban umaṅdnā, v.n. Puberty to show itself; the breasts (of a woman) to be developed
To attain to puberty; to be in full vigour; to bloom
s.m. Youthful strength or vigour; — adj. Strong with the strength of youth
m. (joban-kī mātī, f.) adj. Intoxicated by (the pride of) youth
v.n. To taste or enjoy the pleasures of youth to the full, to revel in the pleasures of youth
m., joban-vatī, f. adj. Young, youthful, juvenile; attained to or arrived at the age of puberty, fit to be married, marriageable; beautiful, handsome.

def: s.m. = joban.
v.t. (prov.) = jovnā; johnā, qq.v.
s.m. A fighter, soldier, warrior, combatant; a brave fellow, a hero.
s.m. Fignting; battle.

ety: jodhā+Prk. प्पणं = त्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Soldiering; bravery, prowess, heroism.
s.m. A warrior &c. = jodhā.
s.f. A wife ( = jorū).

s.m. Joining, uniting, fitting; connection, union; yoking; putting to (horses); meeting, contact; mixture, combination, association; method, means, mode, manner; propriety, fitness, suitability; fixing (an arrow or other weapon); application of a remedy, remedy, cure; consequence, result; occasion, opportunity; (in Astron.) conjunction, a lucky conjuncture, auspicious or fortunate moment; (in Arith.) addition, sum, total; — application or concentration of the thoughts (keeping the body in a fixed posture); abstract contemplation, meditation, devotion, penance; union with the Supreme Being by means of abstract contemplation; the Yoga system of philosophy (as established by Patanjali, to teach the means by which the human soul may attain complete union with the Supreme Being); — the twenty-seventh part of a circle measured on the plane of the Ecliptic (and used in calculating the longitudes of the sun and moon); a division of the moon's path corresponding to the twenty-eight Nakshatras; (in banking) the person on whom a draft or bill of exchange is drawn; — adv. By means of, by reason of, through; in consequence of; on the part of, from
subs: (S. yoga+abh˚), s.m. The manner of life and practice of Jogīs or Hindū ascetics
(S. yoga+āsana), s.m. The posture or mode of sitting proper for profound and abstract meditation
s.m. The precession of the equinoxes
v.n. To practise profound and abstract meditation; to lead the life of an ascetic
s.f. The practice or cultivation of religious abstraction
v.n. To retire from the world, to become an ascetic
s.f. The māyā or magical power of the Yoga, a certain illusionary power which Jogīs are supposed to possess (viz. the being able to assume at will any number of forms of their own body)
s.m. A kind of fish, Silurus ascita
s.f. A kind of sleep (supposed to be peculiar to devotees) which admits of the full exercise of the mental powers, a state of half-contemplation, half-sleep; light sleep, wakefulness
s.m. A menstruum or medium for mixing medicines; — s.f. Alkali; quicksilver

jogeś, jogeśvar (S. yogā+īś˚), s.m. A master of the Yoga; any principal sage or Jogī; one who has obtained superhuman faculties, a magician; a teacher of sacred science; a deity; the object of devout contemplation; an adorer, a devotee; — an epithet of Krishṅa, of Śiva, and of Yājnavalkya.

ety: Prk. जोग्गो, or जोग्गओ = S. योग्यः, or योग्य+कः
def: adj. Useful, serviceable: fit, becoming, suitable proper, appropriate; apposite; applicable; fit for, qualified for; able, capable of, adequate; advisable; (in comp. jog answers to -able, -worthy, &c.; e.g. bar-jog, lit. 'fit for a bridegroom'; marriageable).

ety: Prk. जोग्गो, or जोग्गओ = S. योग्यः, or योग्य+कः
def: adj. Useful, serviceable: fit, becoming, suitable proper, appropriate; apposite; applicable; fit for, qualified for; able, capable of, adequate; advisable; (in comp. jog answers to -able, -worthy, &c.; e.g. bar-jog, lit. 'fit for a bridegroom'; marriageable).
v.t. To take care of, to guard, tend ( = jugānā, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. jog.
s.m. See s.v. jog.

s.m. One who performs the kind of religious exercise called jog, a contemplative saint; a devotee, an ascetic, a hermit; one supposed to have obtained supernatural powers, a magician, a conjurer; — name of a particular mixed caste who are commonly weavers (this caste do not burn but bury their dead; and the women are sometimes buried alive with the corpses of their husbands).
s.f. The wife of a jogī; a female devotee or performer of jog.
s.f. A female demon or being of magical power (created by and attendant on Durgā), a witch, a sorceress; (in Astrol.) a spirit ruling periods of good and ill luck.
s.f. (old H.) = joginī.
s.m. The colour worn by jogīs, a reddish-yellow colour; ochre (syn. geru); — name of a rāginī or musical mode; a kind of pigeon.
s.f. Suitableness, propriety, fitness, appropriateness, consistency; ability, capability.
s.m. (prov.) = jog, q.v.
adj. = jogā, q.v.
s.f. = jogtā, q.v.
s.m. Looking longingly at, gazing at, beholding
subs: adj. Worthy to behold, worth seeing (e.g. Mohan-kau mukha sohan, johan-jog).

def: v.t. To salute, to address reverentially, to make obeisance to.

ety: joh˚ = Prk. जोएह(इ), or जोअ(इ), or जोए(इ); S. ज्योत्स्नय(ति), fr. ज्योत्स्न
def: v.t. To see, look at, behold, gaze at; to look longingly or eagerly at; to look for, expect, watch for ( = apekshā karnā; intiz̤ār karnā).

ety: fr. justan; see jū or jo, and just
def: s.f. (used in comp.), Seeking, searching.
s.m. A measure of four kos, q.v.; a distance of from five to nine miles.
s.f. = joṅk, q.v.
s.f. Weighing; weight; measurement; a weight.
s.f. Weighing; measuring; the weighman's fee or perquisite ( = jukhāʼī).
adj. Weighed, determined by weighing; of weight.
v.n. To run the risk (of, -kī), to venture, risk, hazard; to suffer loss
subs: v.n. To run a risk; to be placed in jeopardy, to fall into danger
v.t. To risk; to place in jeopardy, to endanger.

def: adj. & s.m. Involving danger or risk, venturesome, perilous, hazardous; — a venturesome person, one who risks.
v.t. To weigh; to measure; to estimate, rate, take the measure or amount of (one's abilities, &c.).
s.f. = jokhim, q.v.
v.t. = jokhnā, q.v.
s.f. Partner, fellow, companion, &c. = joṛī, q.v. (used chiefly in comp, e.g. ham-jolī, q.v.).

ety: Prk. जवडु (by change of ḍ to n), fr. जेव, fr. S. यावत्
def: adv. As, like; when ( = jo and jab, q.v.)
subs: adv. As before, in statu quo; in some way or other, anyhow
jon-tyoṅ kar or karke, adv. By some means or other, by any means, somehow, anyhow; as it could be done; with much difficulty

jūṅ-jūṅ, adv. As; as far as, as long as; as by degrees; — joṅ-kā toṅ, jūṅ-kī tūṅ, adj. & adv. Unaltered, untouched, perfect, whole, the very same; — as originally, as it was, in statu quo, the same as ever, precisely the same; as in fact, as in reality
v.t. To preserve or keep unaltered; to let alone; to keep safe and sound; to maintain intact, to uphold
adv. As, like; as possible, in any way
joṅ-hīṅ, adv. The very moment that, just as soon as, no sooner; just as fast as; exactly as if.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

def: s.m.f. The womb, uterus, vulva, vagina, pudendum mulieris
subs: (S. yoni+arśas), s.m. A fleshy excrescence in the vulva, menorrhagia or prolapsus uteri
s.f. The upper part of the vulva, the point of union of the labiœ.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

def: v.t. = jownā = johnā, q.v.
s.m. Aloe wood, agallochum.
s.m. Aloe wood, agallochum.
s.m. Aloe wood, agallochum.
adv. See s.v. joṅ.
s.m.f. = jon, joni, q.v.
s.f. A leech
adv. See s.v. joṅ.
s.f. See s.v. jon, joni.

s.m. Joining, junction, union, connection, join, joint, soldering; conjunction; accretion; combination; society; sum, total; (in Arith.) the rule of addition, addition; (in Alg.) plus; a patch, a seam; a pair, match, equal, mate, partner; an invention, fabrication, concoction; a plot, a trick
subs: (-meṅ), To fit (in) a join, to dovetail
s.m. Joining; joint; contrivance, mechanism; device, craft; — joṛ-toṛ karnā, To contrive, devise, scheme, exercise (one's) skill or ingenuity; to plot (against)
s.m. Scraping together, collecting by small quantities; savings; scraps
adj. Jointed, having joints; (in Bot.) compound
s.f. (Math.), Harmonical progression: joṛ-ghaṭāʼo-laṛī, (Math.) Arithmetical progression
(-meṅ), To put a patch or piece (in); to cast up, to add up a sum.

def: adj. = zorāwar.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ज्वर (jvará).

ety: jor˚ = joṛ˚, rt. of joṛnā, q.v.+S. उ+का
def: s.f. A wife
subs: jorū-kā mazdūr, jorū-kā murīd, s.m. A wife's slave, an uxorious husband; a hen-pecked husband.

def: s.m. Joining, connection; a pair, couple; one of a pair, fellow, mate, match, counterpart; partner, consort (wife or husband); a pair of shoes; a suit (of clothes); — alchemy; alloy
subs: v.n. To form or mix alloy, to alloy.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Joined; mended; patched, &c.
s.f. The act of joining, &c.; the business of joining, mending, &c.; the price of joining, &c. ( = juṛāʼī, q.v.).
s.m. Joining; collecting, &c.; that which joins or binds, &c.; solder; rennet
subs: s.m. One who joins or mends, &c., a joiner, a mender; one who collects or amasses, &c.; one who adds or sums up, &c. (see joṛnā).

def: s.f. = joṛī, q.v.
s.f. A pair, couple; a fellow, assessor, mate; partner, consort; match, equal, counterpart; — a carriage and pair; (local) a pond, tank; — adj. fem. of joṛā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Twin; connote; — a twin; a fellow.

ety: joṛ(nā)+Prk. अडओ; S. अ+र or ल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Twin; (in Math. & Bot.) conjugate; — a twin
v.t. = joṛnā, q.v.

ety: joṛ˚ = Prk. जोड(इ) or जोडे(इ) = S. जोटय(ति) rt. जुट्
def: v.t. To join, unite, connect, bind, tie, attach (to, -se); to mend, patch; to cement, solder; to stitch together; to set (a bone, &c.); to bind up, heal, or comfort (a broken heart, &c.; e.g. ṭuṭe dil-kā joṛnā); to yoke; to harness ( = jotnā); to match, mate, pair; to add together, cast up, sum up; to reckon, calculate, compute; to bring or get together, to collect, to assemble; to accumulate, amass, to scrape together, to save up; to consolidate; to coagulate; to compose (verses, &c.); to make up (a fire); to brighten up, to light (a lamp); to contrive, scheme, devise, plan; to invent, fabricate, concoct
s.f. Counting, adding; calculation, computation, reckoning; the sum total.

ety: rt. of joṛnā+wāṅ = Prk. अओ (with w inserted) = S. अ+कः
def: s.m.f. A twin; a pair, mate, companion, consort (see juṛwāṅ).

ety: prob. Zend yukhsh, fr. yuz = S. युज्
def: s.m. Boiling, ebullition; effervescence; heat, excitement, passion, emotion; lust; fervour, ardour, zeal; vehemence; enthusiasm; frenzy
subs: s.m. The ardour or fire of youth
josh-o-ḵẖarosh, s.m. Noise, tumult, din; excitement, working of passion, anger, passion, rage
s.m. Ebullition of the blood; full-bloodedness, plethora; maternal or fraternal affection
s.m. A breaking out or eruption of the mouth
(-ko), To cause to boil, to boil
josh-zanān, adj. Boiling up, in a state of ebullition
v.n. To boil, to bubble up

josh-meṅ ānā, v.n. To begin to boil, to boil, bubble up, to ferment; to swell, overflow; to chafe, rage; to be heated; to be agitated (as the waves of the sea); to be lustful; to be moved, be touched, be overcome with emotion; to be in a frenzy; to be inspired
v.t To rouse one's anger, &c.; to enrage, excite, &c. (see josh).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جوش (jōš).

def: part. (of joshīdan; see josh), Boiling, bubbling up; foaming (as waves).

ety: imperf. part. of joshīdan; see josh
def: s.m. A decoction (syn. kāṛhā)
s.m. An astronomer; an astrologer; — a low caste of Brāhmans employed in casting nativities.
s.f. Ebullition; heat; fervour; emotion; violent desire (see josh).
s.f. A woman, female; a wife.
s.m. = joshī, q.v.
s.m. The name of a tribe of Hindūs (see joshī).

ety: Prk. जोत्तं; S. योक्तं, rt. युज
def: s.f. Fastening, tie, band, cord, rope, strap, trace (of a carriage, &c.); the strap or cord that fastens the yoke of a plough, &c. to the neck of the ox; a yoke; the rigging of a ship; — (v.n. of jotnā, q.v.) yoking (oxen to a carriage, or to a plough, &c.); — ploughing, tillage, cultivation, husbandry, agriculture; cultivated land, a cultivated tract; the holding or tenure of a cultivator; the rent paid by a cultivator
subs: s.f. The land cultivated and the assessment paid by a cultivator
adj. Fit for cultivation, culturable, arable
s.m. Plougher, tiller, husbandman, one cultivating land
s.f. A statement in the public account of the revenue leviable on land left uncultivated
s.f. The lands of cultivating proprietors (syn. ḵẖud-kāsht).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jotta), from Sanskrit योक्त्र (yóktra, “rope, thong, halter”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *yáwktram, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yáwktram, from Proto-Indo-European *yéwg-tro-m, from *yewg- (“to yoke”).

ety: S. जकुटं; or Prk. जुट्टं = S. युक्तं; — see juṭnā
def: s.m. A pair (as of oxen used in a plough, &c.); one of a pair, a match, peer, fellow, companion, mate ( = joṛā); — adj. Equal, even (opp. to odd); — joṭ bāṅdhnā (-kā), To form a pair, to pair, match.

ety: S. ज्योति, or द्योत+इका, rt. द्युत्
def: s.f. Light, brightness, brilliancy, lustre, splendour; sunshine, sunbeam; a ray of light; flame of a candle or lamp; a light, a lamp; — a glance of the eye, the faculty of seeing, vision, sight; (met.), soul, spirit
subs: jot ćaṛhānā, v.n. To light a lamp (in a temple), to place a lighted lamp (before an idol)
(S. jyotis+swarūp), adj. Luminous, radiant, resplendent, effulgent, glorious; an epithet of the Deity
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jotta), from Sanskrit योक्त्र (yóktra, “rope, thong, halter”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *yáwktram, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yáwktram, from Proto-Indo-European *yéwg-tro-m, from *yewg- (“to yoke”).

ety: jot˚, q.v.+ā = iʼā = iwā = Prk. इअव्वअं or एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. A ploughman, cultivator, husbandman, agriculturist
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jutta, “joined”), from Sanskrit युक्त (yukta, “joined, yoked”) or युक्तक (yuktaka, “pair”).

ety: Prk. जोत्तअं; S. योक्त+कं; see jot
def: s.m. 'A tie-wall,' a partition wall.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jutta, “joined”), from Sanskrit युक्त (yukta, “joined, yoked”) or युक्तक (yuktaka, “pair”).

def: s.m. = joṭ, and joṛā, qq.v.
part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Ploughed, tilled; — cultivated land.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jutta, “joined”), from Sanskrit युक्त (yukta, “joined, yoked”) or युक्तक (yuktaka, “pair”).

def: s.f. Ploughing, &c. = jutāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Ploughing, &c.; — a ploughman = jotār

ety: jotā, q.v.+r = Prk. डो = S. रः
def: s.m. A ploughman, &c. = jotā, q.v.
adj. Fit for cultivation; arable ( = jutāʼū, q.v.).

ety: Prk. जोत्तिआ; S. योक्ति; see jot
def: s.f. The string which suspends the scale of a balance; the strap or cord which fastens the yoke of a plough, &c. to the neck of the ox.
s.f. = jot, 'light,' q.v.
s.m. (Old H.) = jotish, q.v.
s.m. = jotikh = jotish, q.v.
s.m. = jotish, q.v.
s.m. = jotishī, q.v
s.m. = jotishī, q.v
s.m. Astronomy; astrology; — a kind of spell.
s.m. Astronomy; astrology; — a kind of spell.
s.m. An astronomer; an astrologer.
s.m. A ploughman, husbandman, &c. = jotā, jotāṛ.
s.m. = joṭ, joṭā, joṛā, q.v.

ety: जोत˚ jot˚ = Prk. जोत्त(इ) or जोत्ते(इ) = S. योक्तयं(ति), fr. योक्त
def: v.t. To yoke (oxen to a carriage, or to a plough), to put to (a horse); to plough, till, cultivate, bring into cultivation, to reclaim (land); — to drag, force, or constrain (one) to do a thing; to win or buy (one) over

ety: rt. of jot-nā+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. = jotā, jotār.

ety: v.n. of jonā = jownā = johnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Seeing, looking at, beholding.
v.t. To see, look at, behold, regard.
v.t. To see, look at, behold, regard.

ety: imperf. part. of justan; see just
def: part. adj. & s.m. Seeking, inquiring (or or after); — one who seeks or searches (for), a seeker; an inquirer
subs: (-ke), To seek, search (for), to inquire (for or after)
He finds who searches; seek, and you shall find.

ety: imperf. part. of justan; see just
def: part. adj. & s.m. Seeking, inquiring (or or after); — one who seeks or searches (for), a seeker; an inquirer
subs: (-ke), To seek, search (for), to inquire (for or after)

ety: imperf. part. of justan; see just
def: part. adj. & s.m. Seeking, inquiring (or or after); — one who seeks or searches (for), a seeker; an inquirer
subs: (-ke), To seek, search (for), to inquire (for or after)

def: s.f. Yawning, gaping (cf. jr̤imbha).
s.m. Yawning, &c. ( = jr̤imbhā); bursting open, opening (as a flower), blowing; stretching, expanding; — a kind of animal.
s.m. A yawner; a sort of demon or spirit; name of certain magical formularies for exorcising the evil spirits supposed to possess weapons; name of a spell that produces drowsiness.
s.f. = jr̤imbhā, q.v.
s.f. The plant Mimosa octandra.
s.m. & adv. (Braj) = jī, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

def: s.f. Hunger, appetite

ety: Pehl. gūi; Zend vāidhi, rt. vad; cf. S. वधू
def: s.f. A river, stream; a rivulet, brook.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

r: jū, jo, justan, zāyītan, jad, gad
ety: act. part. of justan = Pehl. zāyītan; Zend, rt. jad = S. gad
def: adj. & s.m. Seeking; seeker (used in comp., e.g. jang-jo, 'Quarrel-seeking,' contentious, &c., see s.v. jang).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

def: s.m. A yoke; the yoke of a carriage or plough
subs: To throw off the yoke, to refuse the yoke; to be tired (of).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀽𑀤 (jūda), from Sanskrit द्यूत (dyūta, “gambling”).

def: s.m. Play, gaming, gambling, dice, playing with dice
subs: s.m. A gambler who sneaks away with his winnings
v.n. To gamble, to play with dice.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀽𑀤 (jūda), from Sanskrit द्यूत (dyūta, “gambling”).

ety: jūʼā, q.v.+S. काष्ठं; or ˚ṭ = Prk. डो = S. टः (for रः)
def: s.m. A yoke ( = jūʼā, q.v.).

ety: jūʼā, q.v.+S. काष्ठं; or ˚ṭ = Prk. डो = S. टः (for रः)
def: s.m. A yoke ( = jūʼā, q.v.).
adj. Young.
s.f. A young woman, damsel.

ety: for A. جبة jubbat, fem.; fr. rt. جبّ 'to cut off'
def: s.m. An outer robe or long cloth coat or gown (of any colour), the sleeves of which reach not quite to the wrist
s.m. Cowardice, pusillanimity.
(inf. n. of جود 'to be liberal'), s.f.m. Liberality, bountifulness, munificence, generosity.

ety: Pārsī jaṭ; Pehl. gvit; Zend vita, rt. vi = S. वि
def: adj. Separated, parted; separate, distinct, away, apart, aside, asunder, absent; different; peculiar; extraordinary (syn. alay)
subs: adj. & adv. Various, distinct, several; — separately, severally, one by one; — judā-judā kahnā (-ko), To describe or name separately, to particularize
(-ko), To separate (from, -se), to part, disjoin, disunite, detach, sever, sunder; to disengage, to distinguish
(-se), To be separated or parted (from); to be separate, be apart (from); to live apart.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جدا (judā, “separate, apart”). Compare Punjabi جُدا (judā) / ਜੁਦਾ (judā), Gujarati જુદું (judũ).

def: s.f. Separation, parting; absence; estrangement, discord
subs: (-meṅ, or bīć-meṅ), To sow discord (between), to bring about a separation, canse estrangement.

def: s.m. The pole-star.
s.f. Separation, parting; absence; estrangement, discord
subs: (-meṅ, or bīć-meṅ), To sow discord (between), to bring about a separation, canse estrangement.

def: s.m. War, battle, fight, conflict, engagement ( = jujh)
prop. n. Name of the elder of the five Pāndav princes ( = yudhishṭhir, q.v.).
s.f. Small-pox.
s.f. A rivulet, brook
subs: s.f. A large river formed by many smaller streams; a large body of water, a flood (cf. juʼār).

def: s.m. Evenness; an even number; a pair, couple, match, mate (in general); a pair of shoes; — adj. Even (not odd, opp. of t̤āq).

ety: perf. part. of juftan; Zend yūta, rt. yu = S. यु
def: adj. & s.m. Bent; wrinkled; running together (the threads in cloth); — a wrinkle; crease; flaw, blemish
subs: (-meṅ), A wrinkle, or crease, &c. to be occasioned (in).

def: s.f. Coupling, pairing (of animals); — a parallel ruler
subs: juftī karnā, v.n. To copulate, to pair (as animals).

def: s.m. A pair, couple, brace; a term used in the game of ćausar when two or more pieces come together on the same square (opp. of a blot).
wety: Borrowed from English jug.

def: s.m. A yoke; — an age, a period, an epoch, a cycle; an astronomical cycle of five years, a lustrum; (in Myth.) an age of the world, a long mundane period of years (of which there are four, viz. Kr̤it or Satya-jug, Tretā, Dwāpar, and Kalī, of which the first three have already elapsed, while the Kalī is that in which we are supposed to live; the first comprises 1,728,000 years; the second, 1,296,000 years; the third, 864,000 years, and the fourth, 432,000 years, of which nearly 5,000 have already elapsed); — a measure of length of four cubits; a symbolical expression for the number 4, and sometimes for the number 12; a very long time or period
subs: jugādarī (S. yuga+ād˚), adj. Very old, ancient, antiquated (man or animal)
(S. yuga+anta), s.m. The end of an age; a destruction of the universe; — adj. For an age, for all time
(S. yuga+ant˚), s.m. & adv. Another epoch; — in the midst of an epoch; from age to age
vulg. jugānjug (S. yuga+anu˚), adv. From one age to another, from age to age, during successive ages, to the ages of ages, for ever and ever, to all eternity
adv. At one and the same time, together, conjointly; at once, suddenly
v.n. To become estranged, to fall out
(-kā), To cause a difference (between friends), to divide, part, or sunder (close friends, &c.)
adv. From age to age, for ever, constantly, perpetually, eternally
adv. With the two hands clasped (as in earnest entreaty)
vulg. jug-bidhi, s.f. The quality or manner of a jug, q.v.
s.f. The laws or economy of a jug.
wety: Borrowed from English jug.

def: adj. See s.v. jug.
s.m. = jugal, q.v.
s.m. The cud.
s.m. A pair, couple, brace
subs: s.f. A pair; two brothers or sisters of about the same age.

def: s.f. Chewing the cud

ety: jugāl˚ = S. उद्+गारय (ति) or गालय(ति), fr. caus. of rt. ग
def: v.t. To chew the cud ( = pugranā).
s.m. = jugal, q.v.
s.m. The clothes washed, ironed, and carefully arranged, as brought home by the washerman; the washing; — washing-day.

ety: jugā˚ = Prk. जुग्गाव(इ) or जुग्गावे(इ) = S. युग्म (rt. युज्+आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To place in order, to arrange; to keep with care, to be careful of, take care of; to guard, tend; — to pair off labour, to lend labour, to assist another in his work in expectation of similar assistance being returned hereafter
subs: (or jugā-kar) rakhnā, v.t. To place in due or proper order, to arrange; to gather, collect; to keep with care.

def: For this and other compounds (not found elsewhere) of which jug is the first member, see s.v. jug.
s.f. = جگت jugat, q.v.

s.f. Connection, union; agreement, concord; application, use, practice, usage; appliance, means, plan, scheme, expedient, contrivance, device, trick, stratagem; way, mode, manner; skill, address, dexterity, ingenuity, art; wit; punning, a pun; alliteration; — emblematical or mystical expression of purpose; the mysteries of asceticism
subs: adj. & s.m. Skilful, dexterous; artful, scheming, crafty; — a sharp, scheming, or crafty fellow, &c. ( = jugatī); one who is addicted to using language with a double meaning; a quibbler; a punster
v.n. To pun; to utter a double-entendre.
wety: From Sanskrit जगत (jagata).

def: adj. = جگتي jugatī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Clever, skilful, dexterous; economical; artful, cunning, crafty; facetious; punning; — a skilful man; an artful or crafty fellow, &c.; a punster ( = jugat-bāz).
adj. & s.m. Clever, skilful, dexterous; economical; artful, cunning, crafty; facetious; punning; — a skilful man; an artful or crafty fellow, &c.; a punster ( = jugat-bāz).
v.t. = jugānā, q.v
s.f. = jugnūṅ, q.v.
s.m. A fire-fly; a glow-worm; a jewel or ornament worn (by women) about the neck.

def: s.m. A fire-fly; a glow-worm; a jewel or ornament worn (by women) about the neck.

subs: (or jauhar) karnā, To kill oneself together with wife and children. (When the Rājpūts are attacked by an overwhelming force, they sometimes slaughter or burn their wives and children, and then sell their lives dearly on the field of battle.)

def: v.t. = johārnā, q.v.

s.m. War, battle, conflict, fight, combat, contest, engagement, struggle, strife.

s.m. A fighter, warrior, combatant, soldier.

def: s.m. = julāh, q.v.

ety: Prk. जुअलाओ (with h inserted) = S. युगल (rt. युज्) +कः; — P. jūlāh, jūlāha, 'a weaver'; jula, julla, 'a ball of thread'

def: s.m. A mixture of syrup and rose-water; a sherbet of sugar and rose-water; — a purgative (syn. mus-hil, the ordinary meaning of the word in India).
v.n. To be united, to meet; to agree, to harmonize (commonly used in connection with milnā).
wety: Urdu verb set جَلْنا (jalnā) جَلانا (jalānā) جَلْوانا (jalvānā) Etymology tree Proto-Indo-European *ǵwelH- Sanskrit ज्वल् (jval) Sanskrit ज्वलति (jvalati) Prakrit 𑀚𑀮𑀇 (jalaï) Urdu جَلْنا Inherited from Middle Hindi جلنا (jlna /⁠jlna⁠/) (c. 1649), from Prakrit 𑀚𑀮𑀇 (jalaï), from Sanskrit ज्वलति (jválati). Cognates Cognate with Konkani जळ्चे (j̈aḷce), Gujarati જળવું (jaḷvũ), Marathi जळणे (j̈aḷṇe), Punjabi ਜਲਣਾ …

def: s.m. The pomegranate flower.
s.m. Sitting; accession (to a throne); — state, pomp.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جلوس (julūs), from Arabic جُلُوس (julūs).

def: adj. Of the reign; dating from the accession (of a monarch); relating to the accession (to the throne)
s.f. 'Friday-eve,' i.e. Thursday.

subs: jumādaʼl-ulā, s.m. Name of the fifth Arabian month
jumādaʼs̤-s̤ānī, s.m. The sixth Arabian month.

def: s.f. The weekly allowance to school-boys, paid on a Friday; the present given on a Friday by school-boys to their preceptor.
s.m. pl. (of jumla), The whole, the aggregate, the sum.
s.f. 'Friday-eve'; Thursday
wety: First attested in c. 1669 as Middle Hindi جمعرات (jm'rat /⁠juma'rāt⁠/), a contraction of جُمْعَہ رات (jm'h rat), from جُمْعَہ (jum'a, “Friday”) + رات (rāt, “eve”), literally “Friday eve”, because as per Islamic and Abrahamic tradition, the day changes at sunset.

ety: imperf. part. of jumbīdan; rt. gaf, or gamf, akin to Zend & S. gam
def: part. adj. Moving, stirring, shaking, vibrating, trembling; tremulous; (rt. of jumbānīdan) causing to move or stir, &c., shaking.
s.f. Moving, movement, motion; shake, vibration, trembling; agitation; gesture
subs: (-ko), To put into motion, to move, shake, stir, &c.
v.n. To get into a state of motion or action, to move, shake, &c.; to become active; to be stirred up, be roused, be excited.

def: s.f. corr. of jumʻa-rāt, q.v.
s.m. A large sand-heap; — a great number of people, a collective body (of men); the populace; a community; a whole people; a republic; — adj. Universal; all
subs: jumhūr-ě-salt̤anat, s.f. A republic; a democracy.

def: s.m. Aggregate, sum, amount, total, whole; (in Gram.) a proposition; a clause; a sentence

s.m. A qualificative or adjectival sentence
s.m. A verbal sentence, a sentence of which the predicate is a verb (with the subject expressed, or included in the verb)
s.m. A parenthetical sentence or clause
(rarely, biʼl-jumla), In short, in substance, on the whole; — min jamla, From, or out of, the whole.

def: s.f. Universality, totality, the whole.

subs: The reckoning of the alphabet by abjad, q.v.

def: s.m. Time ( = jūn, q.v.).

def: s.m.f. A kind of pot-herb, Convolvulus argenteus.

s.m. corr. of جرعه jurʻa, q.v.

ety: for A. جرعة jurʻat, fem.; rt. جرع 'to swallow'
def: s.m. A draught, gulp, sup, sip, drop
part. adj. (f. -ī), Joined, united, attached, appended, &c.

ety: juṛā˚, fr. juṛānā+āʼī; see the preceding
def: s.f. The act of joining, soldering, &c.; the price of joining, mending, &c.

ety: caus. of jurnā; — jurā˚ = jur˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To get, obtain, procure; — v.n. To be or become composed or comforted, to be pacified or calmed

r: juṛānā, joṛnā, juṛnā, jor˚, juṛ˚, ā, nā, jaṛānā
ety: caus. of joṛnā and juṛnā, i.e. jor˚ or juṛ˚+ā+nā; see jaṛānā
def: v.t. To cause to join, cause to adhere; to cause to couple or pair; to cause to be mended, to get (a thing) mended; — to cheer.
v.n. To tear the hair (in token of grief or vexation).
s.m. Procurement.

ety: inf. n. of جرأ 'to be bold'
def: s.f. Boldness, daringness, audacity, temerity, bravery, courage, valour
subs: (-meṅ), To make bold (in), to have the audacity or courage (to), to dare, to presume (syn. dilerī karnā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جُرْأَت (jur'at), from Arabic جُرْأَة (jurʔa). First attested in c. 1741 as Middle Hindi جرات (jrat /⁠jurat⁠/).

ety: contrac. of juṛāwaṭ, not in use
def: s.f. (prov.), Joining, join, annexation.

ety: jurāw˚ = jurā˚, as in jurānā, q.v.+an = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: adj. Obtainable, procurable.
s.m. Twins ( = juṛwāṅ).
s.f. Ague = jūṛī, q.v.

s.f. A small bundle of sugar-cane (the tops of the jūṛī brought home on the Ikādasī of Kātik are kept suspended from the roof of the house till the Holī, and burnt during that festival); — a necklace of small cakes of dried cow-dung strung together (which is cast into fire during the Holī).

ety: see jūṛ, jūṛā; ˚ī = Prk. iʼā = S. ikā
def: s.f. A shivering fit, an ague.
